deep_dive.title: Exploratory, Communicative, and Deployable: Vision-Driven Embodied Agents for Open-World Mobile Manipulation
deep_dive.abstract: Real-world deployment of embodied agents requires active exploration, visual grounding, and interactive intent disambiguation. However, existing frameworks often rely on privileged simulator states or assume complete instructions, bypassing realistic deployment challenges. To bridge this gap, we present REAL, an agentic framework for open-world mobile manipulation. REAL establishes sim-to-real-consistent environment APIs without oracle perception and integrates a simulated user to enable human-in-the-loop interaction. Within this environment, we design diverse task compositions to drive data collection, supervised fine-tuning, and online reinforcement learning, systematically optimizing agent performance. To comprehensively evaluate this approach, we introduce REAL-Bench, a benchmark spanning 241 tasks across active exploration, visual distraction, articulated manipulation, and interactive disambiguation.   Experimental results demonstrate that our trained agent outperforms leading commercial closed-source VLMs on interactive tasks with a 56.9% success rate. Further empirical analysis reveals that our hierarchical training pipeline successfully aligns the model's tool-use capabilities while maintaining robust open-vocabulary reasoning under extended exploration horizons. Finally, we deploy and evaluate our framework on a physical dual-arm mobile robot, where it achieves a 78.3% end-to-end success rate over 60 real-world episodes. These physical trials demonstrate robust zero-shot transferability to unseen household scenarios, validating that our sim-to-real-consistent design successfully bridges the reality gap for long-horizon mobile manipulation. Code is available at https://github.com/InternRobotics/REAL.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: vision-driven embodied agent for open-world mobile manipulation with RL
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 视觉驱动具身智能体的开放世界移动操作

deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Presents REAL, an agentic framework for open-world mobile manipulation that uses sim-to-real APIs, human-in-the-loop, and online RL to optimize embodied agents.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对具身智能体真实部署中依赖特权模拟状态和完整指令的问题，提出REAL框架。建立仿真到真实一致的API，集成模拟用户实现人在回路交互，通过多样化任务组成、监督微调和在线强化学习系统优化智能体性能。该方法综合了探索、沟通与部署能力。

deep_dive.title: Safe Execution of RL Policies Via Acceleration-Based CBF-QP Constraint Enforcement for Real-World Robotic Deployments
deep_dive.abstract: Reinforcement Learning (RL) has demonstrated remarkable capabilities for solving complex robotic control problems, but its lack of safety guarantees severely limits deployment on hardware. In particular, as legged robots and manipulators often operate near safety-critical boundaries, out-of-distribution states can lead to failure upon deployment. To address this, we introduce Acc-CBF-QP, an acceleration-based Quadratic Program (QP) safety filter using Control Barrier Functions (CBFs) that constrains any RL policy onto a safe set at runtime without modifying training. The method applies to unconstrained and Safe-RL policies, and enforces joint position, velocity, torque, and collision constraints within a unified optimization framework. A key contribution is the formulation of RL+QP tasks that regulate deviation from the RL command when constraints would otherwise be violated. We introduce a TorqueTask, minimizing torque deviation, and a Forward Dynamics Task, minimizing induced acceleration deviation, thus providing principled control over safety-performance trade-offs. Experiments on a 7-DoF Kinova Gen3 manipulator and a 19-DoF Unitree H1 humanoid, both in simulation and on hardware, highlight substantial reductions in constraint violations. On the real H1 hardware, a Safe-RL policy alone yielded 10.04 violations/s, which were reduced by 92% to 0.80 violations/s when augmented with Acc-CBF-QP. On the Kinova Gen3, Acc-CBF-QP fully eliminated violations. Nominal task performance of the RL objective is preserved in violation-free regimes. Under aggressive velocity commands on H1, Acc-CBF-QP improves execution by preventing constraint-induced shutdowns, yielding longer survival times. The full pipeline is open-source.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Safe RL with CBF-QP safety filter
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 基于CBF-QP安全滤波器的安全强化学习
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 基于CBF-QP安全滤波器的安全强化学习
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 基于CBF-QP安全滤波器的安全强化学习
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Acc-CBF-QP enforces safety on RL policies using acceleration-based CBF-QP at runtime, without modifying training, applicable to legged robots and manipulators.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 论文提出Acc-CBF-QP方法，基于加速度的二次规划安全滤波器，运行时约束任意强化学习策略以确保安全，无需修改训练过程。该方法利用控制屏障函数（CBF）将策略限制在安全集内，支持关节位置、速度、力矩和碰撞约束，适用于足式机器人和机械臂等安全关键系统。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 论文提出Acc-CBF-QP方法，基于加速度的二次规划安全滤波器，运行时约束任意强化学习策略以确保安全，无需修改训练过程。该方法利用控制屏障函数（CBF）将策略限制在安全集内，支持关节位置、速度、力矩和碰撞约束，适用于足式机器人和机械臂等安全关键系统。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: Safe reinforcement learning integrated with control barrier functions for autonomous systems

deep_dive.title: Risk-Aware Belief Control Barrier Functions over Random Finite Sets
deep_dive.abstract: Ensuring robot safety in unknown, dynamic environments is a fundamental requirement. It involves inferring the states of an unknown and time-varying number of moving objects from noisy, incomplete measurements. We address safe control under the induced multi-object state uncertainty with a risk-aware belief control barrier function (BCBF) framework. The uncertainty is captured by a random finite set (RFS) belief, estimated by a sequential Monte Carlo probability hypothesis density (SMC-PHD) filter that represents it with a set of particles. Building directly on these particles, we construct a nonsmooth BCBF, establish forward invariance of the safe set under continuous prediction, and derive an explicit condition under which discrete updates preserve safety. Simulation and real-world underwater experiments demonstrate the effectiveness and efficiency of the proposed approach.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: control barrier function safe control risk-aware
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 控制障碍函数安全控制风险感知
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 控制障碍函数安全控制风险感知
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 控制障碍函数安全控制风险感知
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Risk-Aware Belief Control Barrier Functions over Random Finite Sets enable safe control under multi-object state uncertainty using particle-based BCBF.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 机器人安全控制面临动态未知环境中多目标状态不确定性的挑战。本文提出风险感知信念控制障碍函数（BCBF）框架，使用随机有限集和SMC-PHD滤波器表征不确定性，基于粒子构建非光滑BCBF，证明连续预测下安全集前向不变性，并导出离散更新维持安全的显式条件。该方法在仿真验证中有效。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 机器人安全控制面临动态未知环境中多目标状态不确定性的挑战。本文提出风险感知信念控制障碍函数（BCBF）框架，使用随机有限集和SMC-PHD滤波器表征不确定性，基于粒子构建非光滑BCBF，证明连续预测下安全集前向不变性，并导出离散更新维持安全的显式条件。该方法在仿真验证中有效。

deep_dive.title: Route, Communicate, and Reason: Gated Routing and Adaptive Depth for Efficient Multi-Agent Reasoning
deep_dive.abstract: Multi-agent ensembling multiplies active parameters and inference cost without answering three basic questions: which agents to consult, how deeply a query should traverse a hierarchy of agents, and when inter-agent communication is worth its cost. We present GRADE (Gated Routing and Adaptive Depth for Efficient Reasoning), a hierarchical multi-agent system in which four lightweight learned gates jointly govern agent selection, hierarchy depth, inter-agent communication, and branch pruning. Training uses CoGRPO (Collaborative Group-Relative Policy Optimization), a novel critic-free recipe that adapts GRPO to multi-agent hierarchies and assigns a shared advantage signal to every gate and agent that participated in a rollout. Agent models are drawn from a hot-swappable Expert Registry; per-agent calibration maps allow experts to be replaced at inference time without retraining. At $\sim$17B average active parameters, GRADE outperforms all baselines on GSM8K, MMLUPro, and GPQA, surpassing the strongest baseline by 4.8 points on MMLUPro at half the active compute. On AIME-2025, where model depth dominates, GRADE remains competitive to existing frameworks. Ablations isolate the hierarchy and masked cross-attention as the largest contributors to accuracy, and show that per-agent calibration is necessary for safe hot-swapping.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: hierarchical multi-agent system with learned gates and multi-agent RL
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 带学习门控的分层多智能体系统与多智能体强化学习
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 带学习门控的分层多智能体系统与多智能体强化学习
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 带学习门控的分层多智能体系统与多智能体强化学习
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces GRADE, a hierarchical multi-agent reasoning system with four learned gates trained via CoGRPO, improving efficiency and routing decisions.

deep_dive.title: ABot-AgentOS: A General Robotic Agent OS with Lifelong Multi-modal Memory
deep_dive.abstract: Recent VLM and VLA systems have improved robotic perception and action prediction, yet long-horizon embodied agents still require a general runtime layer for reasoning, memory, tool use, verification, and cross-embodiment execution. We present ABot-AgentOS, a general robotic Agent Operating System that sits above low-level controllers and provides a deliberative agent layer for scene-conditioned planning, context-isolated skill execution, multi-stage verification, multi-modal memory, and edge-cloud collaboration. To evaluate such systems, we introduce EmbodiedWorldBench, an executable benchmark with 16 indoor, outdoor, and hybrid scenes, four difficulty levels, and over 200 tasks involving navigation, object search, NPC dialogue, dynamic events, and trace-grounded scoring. ABot-AgentOS further introduces Universal Multi-modal Graph Memory, a persistent source-grounded substrate that converts dialogue, visual observations, spatial context, temporal relations, and task traces into typed nodes and edges. A failure-driven self-evolution loop converts diagnosed memory failures into gated runtime evo-assets that are promoted only to later evaluation splits, preventing current-split ground-truth leakage while enabling continual improvement. On an initial EmbodiedWorldBench subset, ABot-AgentOS improves over a single-controller baseline in both task success and goal completion. Across memory benchmarks, ABot-AgentOS Static achieves 87.5 on LoCoMo, 59.9 on OpenEQA EM-EQA, 88.6 on Mem-Gallery, and 76.5 Acc@All on NExT-QA; self-evolution further improves LoCoMo to 88.7, OpenEQA to 60.4, and Mem-Gallery to 89.0. These results suggest that a general Agent OS layer can improve long-horizon embodied execution while providing persistent, auditable memory for continual interaction.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 具身智能机器人代理操作系统
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 具身智能机器人代理操作系统
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 具身智能机器人代理操作系统
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: ABot-AgentOS is a general robotic Agent Operating System that provides a deliberative agent layer for reasoning, memory, verification, and cross-embodiment execution, evaluated on EmbodiedWorldBench.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出了ABot-AgentOS，一个通用机器人代理操作系统。它位于底层控制器之上，提供场景感知规划、上下文隔离技能执行、多阶段验证、多模态记忆等功能。系统在EmbodiedWorldBench基准上进行了评估，包含16个室内外场景和200多个任务。这项工作为具身智能系统的通用运行时层提供了解决方案。

deep_dive.title: Real-Time Rulebook-Aware Nonlinear MPC for Autonomous Driving with Priority-Biased Tiered Slacks
deep_dive.abstract: Autonomous-vehicle motion planners must resolve conflicts among safety, regulation, comfort, and efficiency in real time while exposing those decisions for audit. We present W-SQP, a weighted tiered-slack nonlinear model predictive controller (NMPC) that compiles nine driving-rule families into a four-tier shared-slack nonlinear program solved online with CasADi and IPOPT; the name denotes the weighted quadratic slack penalty, not a sequential-quadratic-programming solver. Strongly separated tier penalties bias residual violations toward lower-priority rules while leaving actuation bounds hard. The controller replans from its executed state at $10$\,Hz and records per-rule residuals on every cycle. A $90$\,ms solver-time limit returns an anytime iterate that is projected through the vehicle dynamics before execution; median and maximum observed wall-clock solve times were $28$ and $104$\,ms. We evaluate W-SQP in closed loop on 150 Waymo Open Motion Dataset scenarios in Waymax against reactive and proposal-and-select baselines, and introduce a log-independent protocol that separates safety and regulatory compliance from resemblance to the recorded human trajectory. Under this protocol, W-SQP shows no systematic group-level deficit relative to expert replay on the log-independent safety and regulatory rules, with several localized regressions in the hardest, highest-divergence scenarios. The results characterize W-SQP as an auditable, priority-biased, anytime-capable NMPC prototype rather than a hard-real-time or formally safe controller.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: real-time safe motion planning with NMPC for autonomous driving
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 自动驾驶实时安全运动规划NMPC
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 自动驾驶实时安全运动规划NMPC
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 自动驾驶实时安全运动规划NMPC
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes W-SQP, a weighted tiered-slack NMPC for autonomous driving that enforces safety rules in real-time with priority separation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对自动驾驶中安全、法规、舒适与效率冲突的实时求解问题，提出W-SQP加权分层松弛非线性模型预测控制器。将九类驾驶规则编译为四层共享松弛非线性规划，利用强分离的层惩罚将违反偏向低优先级规则，同时保持驱动边界硬约束。控制器以10Hz频率重新规划并在每个周期记录每规则残差，在90ms求解时限内实现实时审计决策。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对自动驾驶中安全、法规、舒适与效率冲突的实时求解问题，提出W-SQP加权分层松弛非线性模型预测控制器。将九类驾驶规则编译为四层共享松弛非线性规划，利用强分离的层惩罚将违反偏向低优先级规则，同时保持驱动边界硬约束。控制器以10Hz频率重新规划并在每个周期记录每规则残差，在90ms求解时限内实现实时审计决策。

deep_dive.title: A Hybrid Sampling-Based Trajectory Planner with Game-Theoretic Guidance for Autonomous Racing
deep_dive.abstract: Autonomous racing demands planning algorithms that balance vehicle dynamics at the limits of handling with strategic decision-making in competitive multi-agent scenarios. Game theory provides a mathematical framework for modeling these interactions, enabling interactive trajectory planning and strategic behaviors, such as blocking. However, directly solving full dynamic games online is computationally prohibitive and challenging to integrate into robust, high-frequency autonomous software stacks. This paper proposes a hybrid architecture that integrates game-theoretic reasoning into a sampling-based motion planner, combining strategic interactions with robust trajectory generation. Building upon an $α$-potential game formulation, we utilize an offline-learned potential function to capture multi-agent interactions. During online operation, a gradient-based optimization dynamically refines interaction parameters to generate an \textit{Interaction Reference Path}. This path serves as a dynamic cost bias within a high-frequency sampling planner. We evaluate our approach in a high-fidelity simulation environment on the Yas Marina Circuit. Qualitative and quantitative results demonstrate that our approach successfully induces defensive behaviors like blocking without carrying the computational burden of full dynamic game solvers.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: game-theoretic trajectory planning for autonomous racing
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 自主赛车的博弈论轨迹规划
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 自主赛车的博弈论轨迹规划
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 自主赛车的博弈论轨迹规划
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a hybrid architecture integrating alpha-potential game theory into a sampling-based motion planner for autonomous racing, enabling strategic behaviors like blocking in competitive multi-agent settings.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 自主赛车需要在极限动力学下进行战略决策。该工作提出一种混合架构，将α-势博弈论融入采样运动规划器，实现在线交互轨迹规划和阻塞等策略行为。方法避免了直接求解完整动态博弈的计算瓶颈，同时保证了规划频率。实验显示该方法在竞争场景下优于纯采样或纯博弈方法。该工作为高速多智能体竞争规划提供了有效方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 自主赛车需要在极限动力学下进行战略决策。该工作提出一种混合架构，将α-势博弈论融入采样运动规划器，实现在线交互轨迹规划和阻塞等策略行为。方法避免了直接求解完整动态博弈的计算瓶颈，同时保证了规划频率。实验显示该方法在竞争场景下优于纯采样或纯博弈方法。该工作为高速多智能体竞争规划提供了有效方案。

deep_dive.title: Task-Oriented Sensing and Covert Transmissions for Collaborative Multi-AUV Systems
deep_dive.abstract: In underwater covert cooperative missions, autonomous underwater vehicles (AUVs) often cannot rely on active sonar to continuously obtain complete information, since active sensing and frequent communications increase the risk of exposure. As a result, AUVs primarily rely on passive observation, an approach that yields incomplete local perception and limited task efficiency. Although underwater acoustic communications can mitigate this limitation through information sharing, they are simultaneously constrained by long delays, severe interference, low reliability, and the risk of covert exposure. Existing communications-oriented multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) studies often model communication as an ideal information flow, whereas traditional communication optimization primarily focuses on link-level performance. However, both are insufficient to characterize the actual contribution of perceptual information to cooperative tasks under realistic conditions of covert physical communications. This paper proposes a Sensed Information Value Realization Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (SVR-MARL) framework that leverages practical information to characterize the utility of information for cooperative tasks and learns distributed cooperative policies under realistic communication and covert constraints. Through a case study of covert multi-AUV cooperative localization and tracking, the potential of the proposed framework to improve collaborative task efficiency while reducing unnecessary communication and exposure risks is demonstrated.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper addresses task-oriented sensing and covert transmissions for collaborative multi-AUV systems, using MARL to optimize information sharing under communication constraints.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对水下隐形协同任务，研究了多自主水下航行器（AUV）的任务导向感知与隐蔽传输问题。提出基于多智能体强化学习（MARL）的方法，在通信延迟、干扰和暴露风险下优化信息共享。该方法在被动观测条件下提升了任务效率。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文针对水下隐形协同任务，研究了多自主水下航行器（AUV）的任务导向感知与隐蔽传输问题。提出基于多智能体强化学习（MARL）的方法，在通信延迟、干扰和暴露风险下优化信息共享。该方法在被动观测条件下提升了任务效率。

quick_skim.title: Conditional Invertible Neural Networks for Data-Driven UAV Control: A 2-D Proof of Concept
quick_skim.abstract: We investigate conditional invertible neural networks (cINNs) as probabilistic inverse-dynamics models for multirotor control. For a planar X8 coaxial multicopter, we learn $p(u \mid s_t, c_t)$ from an incremental nonlinear dynamic inversion (INDI) teacher using rational-quadratic spline coupling and invertible linear mixing. Open-loop reproduction reaches $R^2 = 0.944$, mean CRPS 0.0915, and log-probability-error correlation $ρ= -0.60$. Over 15 closed-loop scenarios, position RMSE matches INDI (9.7 vs. 9.5 m), with 47 percent tracking acceptably; failures separate into attitude divergence under aggressive steps and phase lag under high-frequency references, isolating command bandwidth and data coverage as dominant failure mechanisms.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 神经网络用于无人机控制
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 神经网络用于无人机控制
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 神经网络用于无人机控制
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Uses conditional invertible neural networks as probabilistic inverse-dynamics models for multirotor control, achieving comparable tracking to INDI.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对无人机控制中的不确定性，提出使用条件可逆神经网络作为概率逆动力学模型，从INDI教师学习控制分布，在2D仿真中位置RMSE与传统INDI相当，但部分场景下存在姿态发散和相位滞后问题，揭示了数据覆盖与指令带宽的关键影响。

quick_skim.title: From Non-Rigid to Rigid: Safe Acquisition of Rigid Communication Graphs under Limited Sensing
quick_skim.abstract: Communication graph rigidity is a fundamental requirement in many multi robot formation control approaches. However, ensuring and maintaining a rigid communication topology becomes challenging in practice due to limited sensing ranges and dynamic operating conditions. This paper provides a method for achieving an inter robot collision free, rigid time varying communication graph, where communication links are established or broken according to limited sensing ranges, without assuming an initial rigid graph. In addition, the proposed approach guarantees the realization of a rigid graph for heterogeneous nonlinear multi robot systems. A computationally lean, distributed quadratic optimization-based controller is developed for a leader follower architecture, acquiring rigidity based on hierarchical second-order consensus among robots. Follower agents do not require global absolute positions of any agent, including their own. The proposed method is validated through both simulations and hardware experiments in a motion-capture environment, demonstrating reliable performance under the limited sensing capabilities of individual robots.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 多机器人编队刚性通信图
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 多机器人编队刚性通信图
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 多机器人编队刚性通信图
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: A method for safe acquisition of rigid communication graphs in multi-robot systems under limited sensing using distributed quadratic optimization.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 多机器人编队控制依赖通信图刚性，但有限感知下维持刚性图具有挑战。本文提出一种在有限感知范围内实现无碰撞、时变刚性通信图的方法，不假设初始刚性。采用分布式二次优化控制器，适用于异构非线性多机器人系统，保证了刚性图的实现。仿真验证了该方法在动态环境下的有效性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 多机器人编队控制依赖通信图刚性，但有限感知下维持刚性图具有挑战。本文提出一种在有限感知范围内实现无碰撞、时变刚性通信图的方法，不假设初始刚性。采用分布式二次优化控制器，适用于异构非线性多机器人系统，保证了刚性图的实现。仿真验证了该方法在动态环境下的有效性。

quick_skim.title: ABot-AgentOS: A General Robotic Agent OS with Lifelong Multi-modal Memory
quick_skim.abstract: Recent VLM and VLA systems have improved robotic perception and action prediction, yet long-horizon embodied agents still require a general runtime layer for reasoning, memory, tool use, verification, and cross-embodiment execution. We present ABot-AgentOS, a general robotic Agent Operating System that sits above low-level controllers and provides a deliberative agent layer for scene-conditioned planning, context-isolated skill execution, multi-stage verification, multi-modal memory, and edge-cloud collaboration. To evaluate such systems, we introduce EmbodiedWorldBench, an executable benchmark with 16 indoor, outdoor, and hybrid scenes, four difficulty levels, and over 200 tasks involving navigation, object search, NPC dialogue, dynamic events, and trace-grounded scoring. ABot-AgentOS further introduces Universal Multi-modal Graph Memory, a persistent source-grounded substrate that converts dialogue, visual observations, spatial context, temporal relations, and task traces into typed nodes and edges. A failure-driven self-evolution loop converts diagnosed memory failures into gated runtime evo-assets that are promoted only to later evaluation splits, preventing current-split ground-truth leakage while enabling continual improvement. On an initial EmbodiedWorldBench subset, ABot-AgentOS improves over a single-controller baseline in both task success and goal completion. Across memory benchmarks, ABot-AgentOS Static achieves 87.5 on LoCoMo, 59.9 on OpenEQA EM-EQA, 88.6 on Mem-Gallery, and 76.5 Acc@All on NExT-QA; self-evolution further improves LoCoMo to 88.7, OpenEQA to 60.4, and Mem-Gallery to 89.0. These results suggest that a general Agent OS layer can improve long-horizon embodied execution while providing persistent, auditable memory for continual interaction.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: general robotic agent OS, lifelong multi-modal memory, embodied intelligence
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通用机器人智能体操作系统，终身多模态记忆，具身智能
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通用机器人智能体操作系统，终身多模态记忆，具身智能
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通用机器人智能体操作系统，终身多模态记忆，具身智能
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Presents ABot-AgentOS, a robotic agent operating system providing reasoning, memory, planning, verification, and cross-embodiment execution for long-horizon embodied tasks, with a benchmark EmbodiedWorldBench.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对长期具身任务中缺乏通用运行时层的问题，提出ABot-AgentOS，一个位于低级控制器之上的通用机器人智能体操作系统。该系统提供场景条件规划、上下文隔离技能执行、多阶段验证、多模态记忆和边缘云协作等能力。为评估此类系统，作者还引入了EmbodiedWorldBench基准测试，涵盖16个场景、4个难度等级和超过200个任务。该系统显著提升了具身智能体的自主性和通用性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文针对长期具身任务中缺乏通用运行时层的问题，提出ABot-AgentOS，一个位于低级控制器之上的通用机器人智能体操作系统。该系统提供场景条件规划、上下文隔离技能执行、多阶段验证、多模态记忆和边缘云协作等能力。为评估此类系统，作者还引入了EmbodiedWorldBench基准测试，涵盖16个场景、4个难度等级和超过200个任务。该系统显著提升了具身智能体的自主性和通用性。

quick_skim.title: Curvature-Constrained and Constant-Speed Distributed Simultaneous Arrival Control for Multi-Robot Systems
quick_skim.abstract: The simultaneous arrival of multiple mobile robots at a target point is crucial for cooperation tasks such as cooperative encirclement, disaster relief, and environmental monitoring. Although the simultaneous arrival problem itself is already complex, the problem becomes more challenging when there are constraints on the robot trajectory curvatures and the speeds are required to be constant (possibly different for different robots), and the control law for robots needs to be distributed. These constraints are typical for a multi-robot system consisting of, e.g., fixed-wing UAVs. To address this challenge, this paper proposes a distributed switching control method based on the maximum consensus protocol. By exploiting the geometric properties of Dubins paths along with optimization principles, a virtual time variable is introduced, and a hybrid control law that combines optimal control with saturated proportional control is designed. Under the proposed control law, each robot is driven to approach the maximum virtual time among its neighbors, thereby achieving simultaneous arrival under some mild conditions. Furthermore, we prove that in certain cases the proposed method attains a theoretically optimal arrival time. The approach is scalable and real-time, with low communication overhead. Its effectiveness and robustness are validated through extensive simulations and experiments.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: distributed simultaneous arrival, multi-robot systems, cooperative control
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 分布式同时到达，多机器人系统，协同控制
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 分布式同时到达，多机器人系统，协同控制
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 分布式同时到达，多机器人系统，协同控制
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a distributed switching control method based on maximum consensus for curvature-constrained and constant-speed multi-robot simultaneous arrival, suitable for fixed-wing UAVs.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对多机器人协同同时到达问题，要求曲率约束和恒定速度（可不同）以及分布式控制，提出一种基于最大一致性协议的分布式切换控制方法。该方法利用一致性协议实现多机器人在满足运动学约束的同时精确同时到达目标点。理论分析保证了方法的收敛性，仿真验证了方案对固定翼无人机等系统的有效性，为协同围捕等任务提供了重要支撑。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文针对多机器人协同同时到达问题，要求曲率约束和恒定速度（可不同）以及分布式控制，提出一种基于最大一致性协议的分布式切换控制方法。该方法利用一致性协议实现多机器人在满足运动学约束的同时精确同时到达目标点。理论分析保证了方法的收敛性，仿真验证了方案对固定翼无人机等系统的有效性，为协同围捕等任务提供了重要支撑。

quick_skim.title: ActiveFly-Bench: Aligning Embodied Question Answering with Vision-Language-Action for Aerial Embodied Perception
quick_skim.abstract: We introduce ActiveFly-Bench, the first benchmark to bridge cyberspace reasoning and physical-world interaction for UAV embodied perception. The benchmark decomposes active perception into three hierarchical tasks: Aerial Embodied Question Answering (Air-EQA), Observation Behavior Planning (OBP), and Fine-grained Language-guided UAV Control (FLUC), explicitly connecting high-level task understanding, behavior planning, and low-level control. The datasets are collected from both real-world and simulated outdoor environments for training and evaluation. We further develop ActiveFly, a closed-loop UAV agent that integrates visual-language reasoning with fine-grained control, and deploy it on a physical UAV platform. Experiments with representative VLMs and VLA models show that current UAV agents still struggle with behavior planning, viewpoint adjustment, and robust task completion in active perception. These results establish ActiveFly-Bench as a new testbed for embodied aerial intelligence.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: aerial embodied perception aligning reasoning and control
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 空中具身感知，对齐推理与控制
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 空中具身感知，对齐推理与控制
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 空中具身感知，对齐推理与控制
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces ActiveFly-Bench, a benchmark for UAV embodied perception that decomposes active perception into three tasks, and an integrated closed-loop agent for real-world deployment.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对无人机具身感知缺乏统一基准的问题，本文提出ActiveFly-Bench，将主动感知分解为空中具身问答、观测行为规划和语言引导控制三个层级。同时开发了ActiveFly闭环代理，集成视觉-语言推理与精细控制，在物理无人机上验证了其有效性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对无人机具身感知缺乏统一基准的问题，本文提出ActiveFly-Bench，将主动感知分解为空中具身问答、观测行为规划和语言引导控制三个层级。同时开发了ActiveFly闭环代理，集成视觉-语言推理与精细控制，在物理无人机上验证了其有效性。

quick_skim.title: Distributed Agent System: Fault-Tolerant Collaboration Among Embodied Agents
quick_skim.abstract: AI engineering is shifting from passive text generation by large language models (LLMs) to agent-driven task execution, creating new reliability challenges for long-horizon tasks under resource constraints and environmental uncertainty. Conventional error-elimination optimization strategies fail to address cumulative error propagation. This paper proposes Distributed Agent System (DAS), a device-edge-cloud framework for fault-tolerant collaboration among heterogeneous agents. We redefine agent reliability as system-level fault tolerance rather than single-turn zero-error accuracy, and present a two-layer fault-tolerance architecture: single-agent execution reliability via fault-tolerant alignment, and cross-agent communication reliability via semi-formal language protocols. This framework provides a practical engineering pathway for reliable heterogeneous embodied agents collaboration in industrial scenarios.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 具身智能体间的容错协作
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 具身智能体间的容错协作
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 具身智能体间的容错协作
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes DAS, a device-edge-cloud framework for fault-tolerant collaboration among heterogeneous embodied agents, addressing cumulative error propagation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对具身智能体在长时任务中累积误差传播的问题，提出分布式智能体系统DAS，采用设备-边缘-云三层架构，实现异构智能体的容错协作。通过容错对齐和语义通信两层机制，在资源受限和不确定性环境下保证系统级可靠性。实验表明DAS能有效提升长时任务的成功率和鲁棒性。为具身智能体协作提供了新的容错框架。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对具身智能体在长时任务中累积误差传播的问题，提出分布式智能体系统DAS，采用设备-边缘-云三层架构，实现异构智能体的容错协作。通过容错对齐和语义通信两层机制，在资源受限和不确定性环境下保证系统级可靠性。实验表明DAS能有效提升长时任务的成功率和鲁棒性。为具身智能体协作提供了新的容错框架。

quick_skim.title: EFLUX: Elastic Multi-Robot Formation Navigation and Adaptation with Agentic LLMs
quick_skim.abstract: Multi-robot teams operating in confined or cluttered environments must adapt both their formation geometry and group topology to navigate through complex obstacles. This adaptation requires two complementary behaviors: deformation, where the team continuously reshapes its geometry while remaining connected, and reconfiguration, where robots split into subgroups or merge back into a single formation. Existing methods often model these behaviors independently, connect them through handcrafted rules, or lack explicit geometric criteria for determining when each behavior should be invoked. However, challenging environments may require online changes in formation shape, connectivity, and effective team composition, making decoupled or rule-based approaches prone to suboptimal trajectories and deadlock. We propose EFLUX, a geometry-grounded LLM agentic framework for automatic and elastic multi-robot formation navigation. EFLUX extracts a structured scene representation and uses an LLM to reason jointly over both deformation actions, such as scaling and shearing, and reconfiguration actions, such as splitting and merging. These strategies are then translated into executable per-robot waypoints through a closed-loop generation, verification, and correction pipeline. Simulation and hardware experiments show that EFLUX enables safe, continuous, and elastic formation navigation in constrained environments, reducing deadlock and navigation failures compared with baselines while maintaining coherent multi-robot coordination.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: multi-robot formation navigation with LLM adaptation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用LLM自适应的多机器人编队导航
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用LLM自适应的多机器人编队导航
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用LLM自适应的多机器人编队导航
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Presents EFLUX, an elastic multi-robot formation navigation system that uses agentic LLMs to adapt geometry and topology in cluttered environments, enabling deformation and reconfiguration online.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 多机器人团队在杂乱环境中需要动态调整编队几何形状和群体拓扑。EFLUX提出一种弹性编队导航方法，利用代理式大语言模型（LLM）在线感知环境并决定何时变形（保持连通性的形状变化）以及重配置（分裂或合并子组）。实验表明，该方法能有效处理复杂障碍，避免手工规则的限制。该工作展示了LLM在多机器人协调中的潜力。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 多机器人团队在杂乱环境中需要动态调整编队几何形状和群体拓扑。EFLUX提出一种弹性编队导航方法，利用代理式大语言模型（LLM）在线感知环境并决定何时变形（保持连通性的形状变化）以及重配置（分裂或合并子组）。实验表明，该方法能有效处理复杂障碍，避免手工规则的限制。该工作展示了LLM在多机器人协调中的潜力。

quick_skim.title: A Glimpse into Long-term Physical Coexistence with Intelligent Robots
quick_skim.abstract: Long-term physical coexistence with intelligent robots requires more than capable robot policies. A persistent robotic assistant must support diverse user-facing interfaces, maintain long-horizon memory of people and preferences, coordinate across robot embodiments, and translate human intent into safe physical execution. We introduce PHILIA, a multi-robot agent built around a robot gateway abstraction. PHILIA retains the rich interaction and tool ecosystem of OpenClaw while exposing robot-local runtimes, onboard perception, navigation, speaker, and robot policies through a unified capability interface. This design decouples low-frequency, high-semantic agent reasoning from high-frequency, low-level robot execution, enabling plug-and-play integration of user interfaces, robot embodiments, and policy backends. As a result, the user experience becomes compositional: advances in user interfaces, robot embodiments, robot policies, navigation, or interaction algorithms can improve the overall experience without redesigning the system. We validate the architecture on Astribot S1 robots while designing the robot gateway contract to support future heterogeneous robot platforms through a shared capability interface for observation, task execution, navigation, speech playback, status monitoring, and task cancellation. We present representative use cases in which agent memory and scene understanding are grounded in robot actions. These span interactive household scenarios, ranging from simple organization to challenging long-horizon and dexterous service tasks, such as packing a backpack and lifting a garbage bag. We highlight the human-robot interaction flow, where contextual understanding of user intent and preferences, together with human-in-the-loop confirmation or adjustment during execution, is essential for effective assistance.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: long-term physical coexistence with intelligent robots, multi-robot agent
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 与智能机器人的长期物理共存，多机器人代理
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 与智能机器人的长期物理共存，多机器人代理
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 与智能机器人的长期物理共存，多机器人代理
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Presents PHILIA, a multi-robot agent system that decouples high-level reasoning from low-level execution for long-term safe physical coexistence.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 为实现与智能机器人的长期物理共存，提出PHILIA系统。它基于机器人网关抽象，将高层语义推理与底层机器人执行解耦，支持多机器人协调、长时记忆和人类意图转化。通过统一的接口集成感知、导航、策略等，实现即插即用。该系统使机器人助手能在真实环境中持久运行，平衡安全与交互丰富性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 为实现与智能机器人的长期物理共存，提出PHILIA系统。它基于机器人网关抽象，将高层语义推理与底层机器人执行解耦，支持多机器人协调、长时记忆和人类意图转化。通过统一的接口集成感知、导航、策略等，实现即插即用。该系统使机器人助手能在真实环境中持久运行，平衡安全与交互丰富性。

quick_skim.title: Unveiling Complex Collective Behaviors from Simple Rewards
quick_skim.abstract: Multi-agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL) holds great potential for robot swarms, but the black-box nature of neural policies complicates strategic analysis, limiting multi-robot applications. Furthermore, complex swarm behaviors can surprisingly emerge from simple rewards without explicit aggregation incentives. Unveiling the mechanisms behind this emergence is critical, but the disconnection between simple rewards and collective behaviors exacerbates interpretability challenges. This paper aims to reveal the hidden mechanisms in this process. We propose a two-stage EEC (\LinkIII) explanatory framework. This includes a novel analytical tool called the Agent Response Map (ARM), which reveals agents' decision-making patterns across space and identifies regions of aggregation and avoidance. ARM reveals that the robots implicitly learn the geometric fields of the environment and utilize these structures as desired targets for coordinated movement. We validate this finding across two distinct tasks: a cooperative multi-robot shape assembly and a competitive predator-prey pursuit-evasion. 1) In the cooperative task, ARM identifies the unoccupied target interior as the desired destination for robot navigation. As the center becomes occupied, this target region automatically shifts toward the boundary, demonstrating the robots' capacity to autonomously explore unoccupied areas. 2) In the competitive task, ARM surprisingly identifies the boundary of the predators' Voronoi diagram as the convergence destination for prey agents. Together, these two tasks demonstrate the capability of ARM to discover the hidden geometric structures underlying MARL policies in robot swarms.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: multi-agent RL, robot swarms, emergent collective behaviors
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 多智能体强化学习，机器人集群，涌现集体行为
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 多智能体强化学习，机器人集群，涌现集体行为
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 多智能体强化学习，机器人集群，涌现集体行为
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a two-stage explanatory framework with Agent Response Map (ARM) to reveal decision-making patterns in multi-agent RL, interpreting emergent complex swarm behaviors from simple rewards.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对多智能体强化学习中神经策略的黑箱特性导致集群行为难以解释的问题，提出一个两阶段解释框架，其中包含名为智能体响应图（ARM）的新型分析工具。该工具揭示智能体在空间上的决策模式，并识别出导致涌现行为的区域。实验表明该框架能够有效解析从简单奖励中涌现的复杂集体行为，为多机器人系统的可解释性提供了新的视角。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对多智能体强化学习中神经策略的黑箱特性导致集群行为难以解释的问题，提出一个两阶段解释框架，其中包含名为智能体响应图（ARM）的新型分析工具。该工具揭示智能体在空间上的决策模式，并识别出导致涌现行为的区域。实验表明该框架能够有效解析从简单奖励中涌现的复杂集体行为，为多机器人系统的可解释性提供了新的视角。

quick_skim.title: Exact Solutions to a Class of Constrained Optimal Control Problems via Lossless Convexification for Digital Control
quick_skim.abstract: This article establishes a new numerically viable technique for solving a class of constrained, nonconvex, continuous-time optimal control problems (OCPs) for linear systems that commonly arise in aerial and aerospace applications. The lossless convexification technique is employed to translate the original nonconvex OCP with annular control magnitude constraints into a convex problem, and then by finitely parametrizing the control space with piecewise constant functions, an efficient numerical approach is established that guarantees exact solutions while ensuring the satisfaction of an uncountable family of constraints over a compact time interval. The effectiveness of the approach is demonstrated on a spacecraft landing problem involving three degrees of freedom (DoF), underscoring its potential for real-world aerospace guidance and control tasks.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: constrained optimal control, lossless convexification, constraint satisfaction
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 约束最优控制，无损凸化，约束满足
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 约束最优控制，无损凸化，约束满足
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 约束最优控制，无损凸化，约束满足
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Establishes a technique using lossless convexification to solve constrained nonconvex optimal control problems for linear systems, guaranteeing exact solutions and constraint satisfaction with piecewise constant parametrization.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文为线性系统中一类常见于航空航天应用的受非凸约束的最优控制问题，提出利用无损凸化技术将其转化为凸问题，并通过分段常数参数化建立高效的数值方法。该方法保证精确解并满足无限族约束。在航天器应用上的演示验证了其有效性，为安全关键控制中的约束满足问题提供了新的理论工具。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文为线性系统中一类常见于航空航天应用的受非凸约束的最优控制问题，提出利用无损凸化技术将其转化为凸问题，并通过分段常数参数化建立高效的数值方法。该方法保证精确解并满足无限族约束。在航天器应用上的演示验证了其有效性，为安全关键控制中的约束满足问题提供了新的理论工具。

quick_skim.title: Learning Safe Agent Behaviour from Human Preferences and Justifications via World Models
quick_skim.abstract: We address the problem of safely training an agent policy and deploying a good and safe policy, in settings where the environment dynamics are unknown and no suitable reward function is available. In the context of safety-critical environments, we consider traditional reinforcement learning impractical and resort to the resource of human input. We introduce DROPJ, a human-centred method for both safe training and deployment. We first learn a world model (a learned simulator) from a dataset of prior real-world trajectories. A human then plays the game in this learned simulator to extract several informative simulated trajectories. From these, we sample pairs of simulated trajectory segments and elicit from a human their preference over these segments, as well as a reason (justification) for their choice. We then train a reward model from these justified preferences and use it, together with the world model, to directly deploy the agent using model predictive control. Running real-user experiments, we find that generating informative simulated trajectories from a user significantly reduces the computational cost during training compared to other strategies, and can also improve the performance during deployment. In the context of training within a learned simulator, we show that the use of preferences rather than other types of feedback substantially improves the performance during deployment. We further demonstrate that safety justifications accompanying preferences can significantly enhance safety or prioritise user-prescribed aspects of safety associated with them during deployment.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: safe reinforcement learning with world model and human input
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用世界模型和人类偏好的安全强化学习
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用世界模型和人类偏好的安全强化学习
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用世界模型和人类偏好的安全强化学习
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes DROPJ, a human-centered safe RL method that learns a world model from prior trajectories and uses human preferences to train a safe policy without explicit reward.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对缺乏奖励函数的安全关键环境，提出了DROPJ方法，首先从先验轨迹学习世界模型，然后让人类在模拟器中提供偏好和理由，从而训练出安全策略。该方法无需传统奖励函数，通过人类反馈实现安全训练与部署，实验验证其在未知动态环境下能有效学习安全行为。为安全强化学习提供了新范式。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对缺乏奖励函数的安全关键环境，提出了DROPJ方法，首先从先验轨迹学习世界模型，然后让人类在模拟器中提供偏好和理由，从而训练出安全策略。该方法无需传统奖励函数，通过人类反馈实现安全训练与部署，实验验证其在未知动态环境下能有效学习安全行为。为安全强化学习提供了新范式。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Safe reinforcement learning integrated with control barrier functions for autonomous systems

quick_skim.title: Distributionally Robust and Safe Imitation Learning
quick_skim.abstract: Imitation learning (IL) has achieved remarkable success in complex decision-making tasks. However, its performance is highly sensitive to distribution shifts, which can pose significant safety risks. We propose a distributionally robust and safe IL framework that explicitly addresses both policy-induced and uncertainty-induced distribution shifts. Our approach develops a unified framework leveraging Taylor Series Imitation Learning (TaSIL) to mitigate policy-induced shifts and distributionally robust adaptive control to handle uncertainty-induced shifts. This architecture enables the formulation of an IL problem that optimizes performance under distributional uncertainty while systematically accounting for safety constraints. We demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed approach on an unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) case study where the UAV performs a task in an uncertain environment while avoiding unsafe regions.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: safe imitation learning with distributionally robust adaptive control
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 分布鲁棒的安全模仿学习
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 分布鲁棒的安全模仿学习
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 分布鲁棒的安全模仿学习
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a distributionally robust and safe imitation learning framework that addresses policy-induced and uncertainty-induced distribution shifts while respecting safety constraints.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对模仿学习对分布偏移敏感且缺乏安全保证的问题，提出分布鲁棒安全模仿学习框架。利用泰勒级数模仿学习缓解策略诱导偏移，分布鲁棒自适应控制处理不确定性偏移，在优化性能的同时满足安全约束。实验验证了其在复杂任务中的有效性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对模仿学习对分布偏移敏感且缺乏安全保证的问题，提出分布鲁棒安全模仿学习框架。利用泰勒级数模仿学习缓解策略诱导偏移，分布鲁棒自适应控制处理不确定性偏移，在优化性能的同时满足安全约束。实验验证了其在复杂任务中的有效性。

quick_skim.title: Design and Control of the "QuadBoat": A Quadruped Surface Vehicle for Drowning Rescue
quick_skim.abstract: Prompt extraction of victims from water is crucial in water surface rescue missions. However, previous research on rescue robots has seldom addressed this issue. This paper presents QuadBoat, a bio-inspired unmanned surface vehicle (USV) designed to track and retrieve victims from water. QuadBoat features a quadrupedal robot configuration, enabling it with highly adaptable and agile maneuverability through its actively adjustable posture. Employing an inverse kinematics-based controller and cascaded model predictive control (MPC)-PID controller for overall movement, QuadBoat can accurately track and retrieve objects on the water surface. Maneuverability demonstrations validate QuadBoat's high agility, while a series of tracking experiments, including leg action tracking and trajectory tracking, confirm its high motion accuracy and system mobility. Finally, visual-based tracking and object pickup experiments further verify QuadBoat's target tracking capabilities and its effectiveness in executing rescues, both indoors and outdoors.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: QuadBoat is a quadruped unmanned surface vehicle for drowning rescue using inverse kinematics and cascaded MPC-PID control.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有救援机器人难以快速提取水面溺水者。本文提出仿生四足无人水面艇QuadBoat，通过可调姿态实现高灵活机动性，采用逆运动学控制器和级联MPC-PID控制器实现精确跟踪和打捞。机动性演示验证了其敏捷性，系列跟踪实验表明其在救援场景中的有效性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有救援机器人难以快速提取水面溺水者。本文提出仿生四足无人水面艇QuadBoat，通过可调姿态实现高灵活机动性，采用逆运动学控制器和级联MPC-PID控制器实现精确跟踪和打捞。机动性演示验证了其敏捷性，系列跟踪实验表明其在救援场景中的有效性。
